markdown.markdown: I am currently a Data Science Institute Postdoctoral Fellow at [Columbia University](https://datascience.columbia.edu/people/maya-ogonah/), where I am applying longitudinal data analysis methods to assess the risk of violence in neuropsychiatric populations. I graduated from King's College London with a BSc(Hons) in Psychiatry in 2021. I subsequently enrolled in a DPhil in Psychiatry at the [University of Oxford](https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/courses/dphil-psychiatry), where I developed expertise in psychiatric epidemiology, prediction modelling and evidence synthesis methods under the advisement of [Prof. Seena Fazel](https://www.psych.ox.ac.uk/team/seena-fazel). This webite contains my (current) research interests and a collection of my work such as selected [publications](https://mayaogonah.github.io/research.html) and recent [talks](https://mayaogonah.github.io/talks.html). More details can be found at [GitHub](https://github.com/mayaogonah) and [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=0z5OZqEAAAAJ&view_op=list_works). #### Research interests #### Brief CV + 2025-2027: Postdoctoral research scientist at Columbia University (NYC, United States); + 2021-2025: PhD candidate at the University of Oxford (Oxford, United Kingdom); + 2024-2025: Academic tutor at the University of Georgia at Oxford Programme (Oxford, United Kingdom); + 2021: Health data research intern at University College London Institute of Informatics (London, United Kingdom); + 2020: Honorary assistant psychologist at Maudsley Simulation and Learning (London, United Kingdom) #### Education + 2025: DPhil in Psychiatry from [University of Oxford](https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/courses/dphil-psychiatry). DPhil dissertation _"Violence Perpetration, Victimisation, and Self-Harm in Traumatic Brain Injury An Evidence Synthesis, Epidemiological, and Risk Modelling Approach"_ is available [here](https://ora.ox.ac.uk/objects/uuid:f6872374-96f6-4a32-ab60-364abaefb8cb). + 2021: Bachelors of Science (Hons) in Psychology from [King's College London](https://www.kcl.ac.uk/study/undergraduate/courses/psychology-bsc). BSc dissertation _"Evaluating School-Type Effects on Intellectual Humility: Does the Prevalence of Psychopathic Traits Partially Explain the Relationship?"_
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml: ### About me I am currently a Data Science Institute Postdoctoral Fellow at [Columbia University](https://datascience.columbia.edu/people/maya-ogonah/), where I am applying longitudinal data analysis methods to assess the risk of violence in neuropsychiatric populations. I graduated from King's College London with a BSc(Hons) in Psychiatry in 2021. I subsequently enrolled in a DPhil in Psychiatry at the [University of Oxford](https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/courses/dphil-psychiatry), where I developed expertise in psychiatric epidemiology, prediction modelling and evidence synthesis methods under the advisement of [Prof. Seena Fazel](https://www.psych.ox.ac.uk/team/seena-fazel). This webite contains my (current) research interests and a collection of my work such as selected [publications](https://mayaogonah.github.io/research.html) and recent [talks](https://mayaogonah.github.io/talks.html). More details can be found at [GitHub](https://github.com/mayaogonah) and [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=0z5OZqEAAAAJ&view_op=list_works). #### Research interests #### Brief CV + 2025-2027: Postdoctoral research scientist at Columbia University (NYC, United States); + 2021-2025: PhD candidate at the University of Oxford (Oxford, United Kingdom); + 2024-2025: Academic tutor at the University of Georgia at Oxford Programme (Oxford, United Kingdom); + 2021: Health data research intern at University College London Institute of Informatics (London, United Kingdom); + 2020: Honorary assistant psychologist at Maudsley Simulation and Learning (London, United Kingdom) #### Education + 2025: DPhil in Psychiatry from [University of Oxford](https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/courses/dphil-psychiatry). DPhil dissertation _"Violence Perpetration, Victimisation, and Self-Harm in Traumatic Brain Injury An Evidence Synthesis, Epidemiological, and Risk Modelling Approach"_ is available [here](https://ora.ox.ac.uk/objects/uuid:f6872374-96f6-4a32-ab60-364abaefb8cb). + 2021: Bachelors of Science (Hons) in Psychology from [King's College London](https://www.kcl.ac.uk/study/undergraduate/courses/psychology-bsc). BSc dissertation _"Evaluating School-Type Effects on Intellectual Humility: Does the Prevalence of Psychopathic Traits Partially Explain the Relationship?"_
